hypothesis: In the synthesis of Ca2SiO4, incorporating estimated heating energy (from reaction enthalpies and heat capacities) and purification costs into the economic-efficiency metric will render the higher-thermodynamic-yield route Ca3SiO5+Si3N4 more cost-effective than CaO+SiO2 whenever industrial electricity prices exceed 0.18 EUR/kWh and purification costs exceed 0.005 EUR/g of byproduct.
